name: Ireland
contextBlurb: Ireland's housing portfolio is led by James Browne (Fianna Fáil), Minister for Housing, Local Government and Heritage. The Housing Finance Agency (Amendment) Bill 2025 expands the agency's lending capacity to 6.65 billion euro through 2028. A national rent control limiting rent increases for properties other than new build apartments takes effect from 1 March 2026, alongside rolling 6 year tenancy protections.

legislation.title: Housing Finance Agency (Amendment) Bill 2025
legislation.summary: Amends the Housing Finance Agency's mandate and expands its forecast lending capacity, covering social housing, cost rental, local authority mortgages, housing infrastructure, and retrofitting through to 2028. Total projected lending of 6.65 billion euro to support delivery of affordable and social housing at scale.

legislation.title: National Rent Control and Tenancy Protection Measures 2025
legislation.summary: Government-approved policy measures introduced 10 June 2025 providing enhanced rent controls and tenancy protections for new tenancies. Includes a national rent control limiting rent increases for properties other than new build apartments from 1 March 2026, and rolling 6 year tenancy protections. Measures enacted through expansion of rent pressure zones nationwide on 20 June 2025.

keyFigures.name: James Browne
keyFigures.role: Minister for Housing, Local Government and Heritage
